name: Baked Polenta with Mushroom Ragout
recipeIngredient: 1 package fresh shitake mushrooms, quartered if large
2 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Make the mushroom ragout: In a large deep skillet, cook the onion, garlic, and rosemary in oil over moderate heat, stirring until the onion is softened. Add mushrooms and season with salt, then cook for 10 minutes or until most liquid evaporates. Stir in tomato paste and wine; boil mixture until most liquid evaporates. In a small bowl, stir cornstarch into broth, add to mushroom mixture, and bring to a boil, stirring. Simmer 2 minutes, seasoning with salt and pepper.
2. Bring water and oil to a boil in a large heavy saucepan. Add 1 cup cornmeal gradually, stirring constantly. Reduce heat to low, then add remaining cornmeal slowly, stirring constantly until mixture boils. Remove from heat; stir in butter, half Parmesan, parsley, and season with salt and pepper.
3. Spread one-third polenta evenly in a buttered 13-by-9-inch baking dish. Chill for 20 minutes or until firm. Quickly spread half remaining polenta in a buttered 3-quart shallow baking dish, top with half ragout, then mozzarella. Cover with remaining polenta and ragout.
4. Invert polenta sheet onto work surface; cut stars with star-shaped cutters. Arrange stars decoratively on ragout and sprinkle with remaining Parmesan. The layered polenta may be prepared up to this point 2 days in advance and kept covered and chilled.
5. Preheat oven to 400°F. Bake layered polenta in upper third of oven for 30-40 minutes or until polenta stars are golden.
